title: Ensure Network Access Control List (NACL) changes are monitored

input_row.title: Ensure Network Access Control List (NACL) changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. NACLs are used as a stateless packet filter to control ingress and egress traffic for subnets within a VPC. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for any changes made to NACLs.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring changes to NACLs will help ensure that AWS resources and services are not unintentionally exposed.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> § Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> § Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: 3. aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 4. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: 5. "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = CreateNetworkAcl) || ($.eventName = CreateNetworkAclEntry) || ($.eventName = DeleteNetworkAcl) || ($.eventName = DeleteNetworkAclEntry) || ($.eventName = ReplaceNetworkAclEntry) || ($.eventName = ReplaceNetworkAclAssociation) }" 6. Note the <nacl-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 7. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <nacl-changes-metric> captured in step 4: 8. aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<nacl-changes-metric>]' 9. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 10. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: 11. aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for NACL changes and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: 2. aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name <nacl-changes-metric> --metric-transformations metricName=<nacl-changes-metric>,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = CreateNetworkAcl) || ($.eventName = CreateNetworkAclEntry) || ($.eventName = DeleteNetworkAcl) || ($.eventName = DeleteNetworkAclEntry) || ($.eventName = ReplaceNetworkAclEntry) || ($.eventName = ReplaceNetworkAclAssociation) }' Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 3. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: 4. aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 5. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: 6. aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 7. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: 8. aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <nacl-changes-alarm> --metric-name <nacl-changes-metric> --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.default_value: By default, CloudTrail logs NACL events (e.g., CreateNetworkAcl, ReplaceNetworkAclEntry), but no CloudWatch metric filters or alarms exist. These changes are captured but not actively monitored unless configured.
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_logs_nacl_changes_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **AWS API Calls and CLI Commands:**
   - Use `boto3` SDK to call the following methods:
     - `cloudtrail.describe_trails()` to list all CloudTrail trails.
     - `cloudtrail.get_trail_status()` to check the logging status of the identified trail.
     - `cloudtrail.get_event_selectors()` to confirm the management events included in the trail.
     - `logs.describe_metric_filters()` to retrieve metric filters associated with the CloudTrail log group.
     - `cloudwatch.describe_alarms()` to get alarms associated with the metric filter.
     - `sns.list_subscriptions_by_topic()` to verify subscriptions to the SNS topic.
   
   - Alternatively, the equivalent AWS CLI commands can be used:
     - `aws cloudtrail describe-trails`
     - `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>`
     - `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>`
     - `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>`
     - `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<nacl-changes-metric>]'`
     - `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>`

2. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - List all CloudTrail trails and filter for those that are multi-region.
   - For each valid trail, check the corresponding CloudWatch Logs log group.
   - Check for the metric filter that tracks Network Access Control List changes.
   - Check the CloudWatch alarms associated with this metric filter for alerts on changes.
   - Verify SNS topic subscriptions are active for notification.

3. **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Ensure that at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail is present with logging enabled.
   - Verify that the trail captures all management events and has the correct event selectors.
   - Confirm that a metric filter exists that matches the filter pattern specified to monitor NACL changes.
   - Ensure that at least one CloudWatch alarm is linked to the metric filter and that it has an associated active SNS subscription.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
   - **Pass:** 
     - At least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with logging enabled.
     - Event selectors include management events for NACL changes.
     - Metric filter exists with the specified filter pattern for NACL events.
     - A CloudWatch alarm is linked to the metric filter and the SNS topic has at least one active subscription.
   - **Fail:**
     - If any of the above checks do not return satisfactory results, the control is considered not compliant.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification as Automated is correct. This control can be effectively monitored using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI commands. The use of CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, and SNS for real-time monitoring aligns with AWS best practices for automation.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly accurate. The boto3 methods and CLI commands listed are valid and appropriate for the task. However, there is a minor oversight: the `cloudtrail.get_event_selectors()` should explicitly verify that management events include `ModifyNetworkAclEntry` and `CreateNetworkAclEntry` actions, which are specific to NACL changes.
- The program name, `aws_cloudtrail_logs_nacl_changes_monitoring`, is descriptive but could be more specific by including terms like `event_selectors` or `metric_filters` to clarify the scope of the check.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- **Specificity in Event Selectors:** Ensure that the `cloudtrail.get_event_selectors()` method checks for the inclusion of specific NACL-related events, such as `ModifyNetworkAclEntry` and `CreateNetworkAclEntry`.
- **Program Name Suggestion:** Consider renaming the program to `aws_cloudtrail_nacl_event_selectors_monitoring` to better reflect the focus on event selectors and monitoring.
- **Additional Considerations:** It might be beneficial to verify that the CloudWatch Logs log group retention period is set appropriately to ensure logs are retained for compliance purposes.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-structured and aligns with AWS best practices for monitoring NACL changes. The use of automation through AWS APIs and CLI commands is appropriate and effective.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the automation details to include checks for specific NACL-related events in the event selectors using `cloudtrail.get_event_selectors()`. Consider renaming the program for clarity as suggested above.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
Both AI opinions agree that this control can be effectively monitored using AWS APIs, specifically through CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, and SNS. The necessary API calls and CLI commands are available to automate the monitoring of NACL changes by setting up metric filters and alarms. The automation process aligns with AWS best practices for real-time monitoring and alerting.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
aws_cloudtrail_nacl_event_selectors_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS Resource/Service:** CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, SNS
- **API/CLI Method:** 
  - `boto3` methods: `cloudtrail.describe_trails()`, `cloudtrail.get_trail_status()`, `cloudtrail.get_event_selectors()`, `logs.describe_metric_filters()`, `cloudwatch.describe_alarms()`, `sns.list_subscriptions_by_topic()`
  - AWS CLI: `aws cloudtrail describe-trails`, `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status`, `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors`, `aws logs describe-metric-filters`, `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms`, `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic`
- **Properties to Check:** 
  - Ensure `IsMultiRegionTrail` is true for CloudTrail
  - Verify `ModifyNetworkAclEntry` and `CreateNetworkAclEntry` are included in event selectors
  - Check for the existence of metric filters and alarms related to NACL changes
- **Pass Criteria:** 
  - Multi-region CloudTrail with appropriate event selectors and active metric filters/alarms
- **Fail Criteria:** 
  - Absence of required event selectors, metric filters, or alarms

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**
There were no significant disagreements between the AI opinions. Both supported an automated approach, and the second opinion provided additional specificity regarding event selectors, which was incorporated into the final decision.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Amazon_Web_Services_Foundations_Benchmark_v6.0.0
